chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Crl. Misc. Appl. No.407 of 2022 (Basheer Ahmed v. The S.P Complaint cell and others) __________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge __________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on MA No.8724/2024 2. For hearing of main case 3. For orders on MA No.8725/2022 22.10.2024 Mr. Khalid Hussain, advocate for the applicant. Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG for the State Private respondents in person ========= 1. Deferred. 2-3. It is contended by learned counsel for the applicant that three out of five cheques have been returned to the applicant by the proposed accused who are still harassing and blackmailing the applicant of dire consequences concerning the two cheques which are in their possession. In these premises, he sought direction against SHO PS Shah Latif Town Karachi to record the FIR of the applicant by setting aside the impugned order whereby the application of the applicant under Section 22A/B Cr.PC for the purpose was dismissed by learned IIIrd- Additional Sessions Judge/ Ex-Officio Justice of Peace Malir Karachi. Learned DPG and the private respondents in person by supporting the impugned order have sought dismissal of the instant Crl. Misc. Application. Heard arguments and perused the record. If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that something wrong has been done with the applicant by the proposed accused which constitutes a cognizable offence and for that his FIR is not being recorded by the police, then he has a remedy to exhaust by filing a direct complaint of the incident before the Court having jurisdiction.

Heard arguments and perused the record. If for the sake of arguments, it is believed that something wrong has been done with the applicant by the proposed accused which constitutes a cognizable offence and for that his FIR is not being recorded by the police, then he has a remedy to exhaust by filing a direct complaint of the incident before the Court having jurisdiction. Such remedy if exhausted besides being alternate would be adequate in the circumstances. Even otherwise no illegality is noticed in the impugned which may justify this court to interfere with the same by way of the instant Crl. Misc. Application under Section 561-A Cr.PC, it is dismissed accordingly. J U D G E Nadir*
